text:
Quick purpose: sketch and annotate a short reading + experiment plan that connects recent sparsity/NLP papers to concrete inference cost levers we can try in Redwood. Not a literature review — targeted notes aimed at an engineering sprint (latency/cost first, accuracy second).

High-level thinking (one-liners):
- Sparsity is not a single knob: structured (block/row) vs unstructured pruning vs conditional compute (MoE / routing) have very different engineering and latency implications.
- For serving economics, effective BOPs/token and memory traces matter more than just FLOPs in isolation. Implementation complexity and kernel availability (cuSparse, Triton) decide real cost.
- Weak hypothesis: small/moderate structured sparsity + aggressive quantization yields the best cost-quality tradeoff for 8–32k context models on GPU inference without re-architecting serving stack. Conditional com...

Context:
- Quick personal scratchpad for the 60m bridge sync between Product (Tess), Serving Runtime (Marco), and Applied ML (Arjun).
- Goal: align on the next two-quarter priorities where product wants a single predictable latency target for hosted API endpoints while Applied ML wants to push a quantized 8-bit variant for certain families. Engineering needs clarity on rollout constraints and perf regression gates.
- Current tension: product target = p95 < 120ms for 4k tokens; ML wants to enable 8-bit at model-level to reduce cost but we’ve seen variable latency spikes in canaries.

Meeting goals / success criteria (what I want to walk out with):

text:
Quick context: caffeine-fueled catch-all for today. Focus: short repro of late-morning p99 spike on small prompts, quick triage steps, minimal TODOs so I can sprint on the microbatch scheduler fix after standup.

Summary (TL;DR):
- P99 up ~350ms on hosted-api route /v1/generate for 32-token prompts between 09:30–10:15 PT. Baseline normally ~120ms.
- Suspect: KV cache miss storm combined with aggressive microbatch window increase after autoscaler burst.
- Repro steps below; quick mitigation = revert microbatch-window to 6ms, re-enable per-model prefix cache TTL.

Observed signals:
- Grafana quick check: route gen-hosted-usw2 spike correlates with instance scale-up events (autoscale + burst). CPU fine, but GPU SM util drops to ~40% at spike start, then recovers.
- Logs: many WARNs: "kv-cache: miss_rate 0.92" during window.
- Tracing: requests that hit p99 show cache lookup + redis RTT +...

text:
Morning quick-capture (rambling, prioritised)

Context
- Noticed another small p99 blip during warmup on small-seq workloads (seq_len ~64) after the recent kv-prefetch changes in the allocator branch. Blips reproducible on one perf rig (rig-07) but not on rig-03.
- Hypothesis: cascade of partial prefetches + numa migration + small-batch packing causes short-lived cache churn that temporarily increases token latency.

Fast checklist / what I ran this morning
1) Reproduce baseline:
 - Checkout: runtime@alloc-prefetch-experiment
 - Run microbench: ./scripts/microbench --model llama-13b --seq 64 --warmup 300 --batch-policy continuous --repeat 6
 - Observed p99 spikes at ~60s and ~220s on rig-07 (cpu/mem lines look normal).
2) Capture traces:
 - sudo perf record -a -g -o /tmp/prefetch-perf.data -- ./runtime-bench ...
 - nvprof not helpful here; used nsight-cu for kernel timing (small jitte...

title: Anchored Resetters — probe scratchpad
preview:
Purpose:
Quick personal lab to iterate on the idea of 'anchored resetters' — compact prompt fragments you prepend or inject to reliably steer a conversation back to a safe/observable baseline after divergent model behavior. Goal: make light-weight, few‑token primitives that (a) stop runaway persona drift, (b) remove...
text:
Purpose:
Quick personal lab to iterate on the idea of 'anchored resetters' — compact prompt fragments you prepend or inject to reliably steer a conversation back to a safe/observable baseline after divergent model behavior. Goal: make light-weight, few‑token primitives that (a) stop runaway persona drift, (b) remove user-injected jailbreak attempts, and (c) preserve important session state when desired.

Notes / hypothesis (raw):
- Resetter should be short (< 60 tokens) so cost impact small.
- Must be robust to adversarial user messages between turns (e.g., long instructions trying to re-anchor).
- Two modes: hard-reset (wipes conversational persona & most context) vs anchor-reset (preserve allowed slots/variables but remove behavioral instructions).
- Prefer declarative language over imperative ("You are now X" vs "Do not" tradeoffs). Need tests.
- Consider embedding an explicit prov...

text:
Purpose: quick prep notes for the immediate containment huddle with Eng + CS before formal handoff to SEV-mode bridge.

Context (short):
- Customer X started reporting elevated 5xxs on hosted inference routes (model: gptx-13b-v1) around 2026-03-10 14:20 UTC. Errors spike correlated with long-context generation requests (>2048 tokens).
- Initial mitigation in prod: rate-limit by token-window, disabled streaming responses to that route. Reduced error rate but latency increased for some tail requests.
- Priority: validate root cause (KV cache pressure? kernel OOM?), patch fallback policy, and coordinate customer comms.

Meeting objective (30min pre-bridge):
- Align on facts we know vs unknowns
- Decide immediate mitigation to stabilize SLA (target: restore <1% error rate within 1h)
- Assign owners for data collection + fixes
- Prepare customer-safe statement + next-update ETA

Attendees ...

Purpose / how I use this
- Quick running notebook for one-week sprints: capture metric blips, micro-hypotheses, tiny experiments, and things I want to remember for retros. Not a charter — ephemeral, editable.
- Anchor each week to one north-star micro-KPI + two leading indicators. Avoid more than 3 measurable things.

This week snapshot (week starting 2026-03-02)
- North-star micro-KPI: median 95th-percentile request latency on rtx-pool (50–512 tokens) <= 230ms.
 - Current: 247ms (up from 221ms last week).
 - Delta: +26ms, primary regression window 11:00–14:00 PT.
- Leading indicator A: cache hit ratio on prefix-cache >= 28%
 - Current: 24.6% (down 3.4ppt).
- Leading indicator B: average tokens/session for prod-chat flows (subset A) = 98 tokens
 - Current: 101 tokens (unchanged).
- Cost: estimated per-1M tokens token-cost (gpu-hours + infra) = $175 (est), +3% week-over-week.

title: Adaptive instruction moderation proxy — draft
preview:
Purpose / quick summary:
This is a personal draft for a small edge-side/in-between proxy that mediates instruction-like requests before they hit model servers. The goal: afford cheap, configurable, adaptive moderation and routing (to safety-tuned variants, fallback filters, or human-review) while keeping client late...
text:
Purpose / quick summary:
This is a personal draft for a small edge-side/in-between proxy that mediates instruction-like requests before they hit model servers. The goal: afford cheap, configurable, adaptive moderation and routing (to safety-tuned variants, fallback filters, or human-review) while keeping client latency and cost low.

Motivation and constraints:
- We need fast local checks (client-side or near-edge) that can block/soft-fail obviously risky instructions without an expensive round-trip to the main safety pipeline.
- Avoid doubling token cost for customers: prefer metadata checks and compact fingerprints where possible.
- Support gradual policy tuning: thresholds that learn from downstream signals (qa labels, human review verdicts, safety evals).
- Must be compatible with hosted + dedicated + private deployments (pluggable enforcement layer).
- Minimal trust assumptions: ...

title: Briar Ventures - ops prospect onramp journal

title: Client-side hypotheses lab: lightweight digest
preview:
Context
---
Personal scratchpad for a small client-side runtime that stages prompts and warmup for cost-sensitive mobile / edge apps. Purpose is to prototype an API shape + lightweight on-device heuristics that play nicely with Redwood hosted/Dedicated routing (fallthrough) and telemetry. Not a product spec — more a...
text:
Context
---
Personal scratchpad for a small client-side runtime that stages prompts and warmup for cost-sensitive mobile / edge apps. Purpose is to prototype an API shape + lightweight on-device heuristics that play nicely with Redwood hosted/Dedicated routing (fallthrough) and telemetry. Not a product spec — more a runnable thought-experiment.

Goals
- Keep client logic tiny and deterministic (no ML on-device if possible).
- Expose clear signals to server for routing/batching decisions.
- Let client trade latency for token-cost predictably (configurable).
- Fail-safe: server can ignore client hints.

Non-goals
- Full privacy model (assume user consents; offload privacy bits to product team).
- Complex on-device model selection or quantization.

High-level idea
- Client keeps a small "staging buffer" for a conversation (prefix + recent messages).
- When app expects user to call the LL...

text:
TL;DR: quiet night, two resurrected flaps: short latency spike on inference-gateway around 03:10 UTC (KV cache thrash), and a noisy Dedicated pool autoscaler blip that recovered after restart. Leaving a few small follow-ups + temporary mitigations.

Quick summary of what I did:
- 01:40 UTC: rolled forward a small kernel fallback patch to staging to test for OOM on rtx-40 variant (PR referenced).
- 03:12 UTC: observed 95th latency jump from 220ms -> 1.6s on inference-gateway, coincident with elevated redis misses in kv-cache. Enabled prefix-caching override (temp) and turned on route-fallback to model-variant: small-7b for high-latency sessions.
- 04:05 UTC: Dedicated autoscaler made a rapid scale-down then scale-up (suspect metric wobble from prom exporter). Restarted autoscaler-manager pod (k8s pod/restart).

Open timeline (detailed):
- 03:05 UTC - small drop in kv-cache hit ratio (f...

preview:

text:
Context:
- Sporadic 401s and 429s observed from mobile app (iOS release channel) calling /v1/generate and /v1/chat/completions between 2026-03-02 and 2026-03-09. Desktop and server-to-server traffic largely unaffected.
- Users report transient failures ~1-3s into a session or immediately after app background/foreground.
- Hypothesis: short-lived client key + network retries causing quota bursts, or a bug in auth cache eviction.

Raw traces / snippets (redacted keys and ids):
- Example curl that reproduces locally (simulated):
 curl -v https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate 
 -H "Authorization: Bearer <REDACTED_KEY>" 
 -H "Content-Type: application/json" 
 -d '{"model":"redwood-small","input":"say hi"}'

- 401 response body seen in mobile logs:
 2026-03-05T18:21:12.482Z request_id=3a9f8b2a status=401 path=/v1/generate src=mobile-ios-1
 response: {"error":"invalid_api_key","message":"API ke...

- Define minimal repeatable evidence paths for controls touching private deployments, model hosting, and key management. Prioritize controls that require infra changes (token rotation, HSM access logging) vs docs/process (onboarding, approvals).

The goal is: fast triage, consistent short-term heuristics, low-risk changes, and clear escalation signals for anything that looks like systemic drift or poli...
text:
Purpose:
This is a lightweight, mostly personal/on-call cheat sheet for labelers who pick up low-volume queues (weekends, holidays, cross-timezone slack-ins). The goal is: fast triage, consistent short-term heuristics, low-risk changes, and clear escalation signals for anything that looks like systemic drift or policy gaps.

Scope and assumptions:
- Applies to conversational/text labeling tasks (intent, slot spans, PII redaction, entity linking).
- You have access to the labelling UI and the minimal metadata (source, timestamp, prior labels if any).
- No heavy schema changes or rework on weekends — opt for containment and notes.

Quick flow (5–10 min per ticket if straightforward):
1) Read utterance + context (1–2 turns). If it's obviously noise (garbage tokens / long web dump), mark as NOISE and add short reason.
2) If clear labelable case, apply standard label. Leave a 1-line ration...

text:
Purpose: quick, portable pass for the next person taking coverage — not a full runbook. Keep this on your phone.

TL;DR (60s):
- If paged: check incident title in PagerDuty -> open the Linear ticket if linked.
- Verify alert: hit /metrics/alerts dashboard and the service health widget for redwood-serving.
- If CPU/GPU saturation and latency spike: toggle fallback-route to small-models (route: /fallback/compact) and notify #sre-night.
- If OOMs or CUDA errors: restart the affected worker group (cmd below), leave 1 instance warm, watch KV cache hit-rate.

Quick commands (use with care):
- List running workers: gcloud compute instances list --filter=\"labels:rw-worker\" --format=\"table(name,status,zone)\"
- Restart worker group (temp): terraform apply -target=module.worker_pool[\"night-temp\"] -var='region=us-west1' (note: this is a blunt hammer, use only when evicted jobs > 5 and metri...

title: Anchored Context TTL + Revocation — personal jot
preview:

Goals (high level):
- Reduce repeated cl...
text:
Context: quick brain dump about a lightweight server-side 'anchor' concept — small named pieces of user context that callers can reference by id instead of re-sending large prompt payloads every request. Focus: TTL semantics, revocation, API ergonomics, and graceful rollout.

Goals (high level):
- Reduce repeated client token cost by letting client store/upload context once and reference by id.
- Make anchored items cheap to cache and evict; predictable cost and SLOs.
- Provide clear semantics for staleness and revocation so product teams can rely on anchors for safety-sensitive snippets.

Rough mental model:
- Anchor = immutable blob (or small set of blobs) uploaded once, gets id (anchor_id).
- Anchor metadata: owner_tenant, created_at, ttl_seconds (optional), freshness_hint (client-suggested version), tags.
- Anchor may be ephemeral (short TTL) or persistent (long TTL).
- On inferen...

title: Apportionment-led pricing sketch
preview:
Goal: quick personal scratchpad for an apportionment-first pricing idea. Try to keep it conversational — rough math only.

Core idea (1-liner): charge customers a small pool of credits where credits are apportioned at request-time across feature axes (generation tokens, embedding calls, rerank calls, SLO uplift) so ...
text:
Goal: quick personal scratchpad for an apportionment-first pricing idea. Try to keep it conversational — rough math only.

Core idea (1-liner): charge customers a small pool of credits where credits are apportioned at request-time across feature axes (generation tokens, embedding calls, rerank calls, SLO uplift) so product teams can buy a single commitment but we can measure and price the true cost drivers per request.

Why this?
- Hosted customers hate 10+ line items. One commitment number (credits) is simple for sales.
- But we need cost fidelity so heavy embedding/rerank usage still tracks to unit economics.
- Apportionment: every request is scored into buckets (generate / embed / rerank / mem-restore / latency-sla uplift) and a predetermined credit weight is charged. Pool drains accordingly.

Assumptions (quick):
- GPU amortized cost: $6.5 / GPU-hour (multi-tenant avg after reserv...

title: Context-boundary regression notes (token-resume artifacts)
preview:
Summary:
I noticed a recurring failure mode when resuming generation after an interrupted stream / resumed KV-cache context. Short description: the model emits plausible-looking but semantically inconsistent continuations when the prior sequence ended in the middle of a token or at a tokenization boundary that diffe...
text:
Summary:
I noticed a recurring failure mode when resuming generation after an interrupted stream / resumed KV-cache context. Short description: the model emits plausible-looking but semantically inconsistent continuations when the prior sequence ended in the middle of a token or at a tokenization boundary that differs between quantized/FP variants. This seems more frequent on 4-bit quantized variants and for models with mixed BPE/tokenization (some open models we use).

Why this matters:
- Customer impact: rare but visible hallucinations in chat-worker workflows where streaming/resume is used for long-running conversations or reconnects.
- Hard to catch with random sampling; shows up in real-time streaming replays and when prefix caching is enabled.

Repro primer (minimal):
- Model: redwood/llamaX-13b-q4f16 (approx), runtime: dedicated1 (local repro via runtime/dev image)
- CLI repro ...

text:
Purpose:
This is a personal scratchpad for quick ad-hoc checks around contradictory outputs from our hosted-chat models. Intention is lightweight: collect interesting failure snippets, sketch a tiny scoring rubric I can run through when spot-checking a batch, and jot hypotheses for regressions. Not formal — use to seed a ticket or a narrow eval later.

How I use this doc:
- Copy/paste any weird Q->A pairs here with model name + timestamp.
- Tag whether it's partial hallucination, direct contradiction (model saying A then B in same session), or external-knowledge mismatch.
- Quick 0–3 micro-rubric score for severity and reproducibility.
- Note any upstream prompts, system messages, or function-calls involved.

Micro-rubric (fast, for spot checks):
- 0 = clear accept: no contradiction, answer internally consistent.
- 1 = minor inconsistency: ambiguous phrasing; does not change primary c...

title: Adversarial prompt surface mitigation sketch
preview:
Notes to self: rough design for a lightweight, pluggable mitigation layer for adversarial prompt inputs. This is intentionally coarse — think of it as the scratchpad to drive an API + config proto for client+ingress integration.

Goals:
- Provide a tiny, deterministic API that clients (web, mobile, sidecars) and ing...
text:
Notes to self: rough design for a lightweight, pluggable mitigation layer for adversarial prompt inputs. This is intentionally coarse — think of it as the scratchpad to drive an API + config proto for client+ingress integration.

Goals:
- Provide a tiny, deterministic API that clients (web, mobile, sidecars) and ingress can call to decide whether to allow, rewrite, or escalate a prompt before it hits the model.
- Keep latency impact sub-10ms for common checks; degrade gracefully to async evaluation when needed.
- Expose explainability signals for downstream QA and audits.
- Make policy configurable per-tenant, per-route, per-model-version.

Threat model (quick):
- Prompt injection attempts trying to override system instructions or exfiltrate PII.
- Maliciously formatted inputs that attempt to exploit tokenization quirks or model behavior (e.g., multi-line re-instructions).
- Social en...

title: Client instruction wrapping & verification — scratchpad
preview:
Context:
This is a personal sketch for a small runtime-level addition: a light-weight client-side 'instruction wrapper' that ensures certain critical instructions survive client-side transformers, are auditable at the server, and can be validated before being applied to session state or KV caches. Motivation comes f...
text:
Context:
This is a personal sketch for a small runtime-level addition: a light-weight client-side 'instruction wrapper' that ensures certain critical instructions survive client-side transformers, are auditable at the server, and can be validated before being applied to session state or KV caches. Motivation comes from a few incidents where client-side SDKs injected or rewrote system instructions (for localization or heuristics) and servers couldn't distinguish benign wrapping from accidental/suspicious mutations.

Goals:
- Provide a minimal wire-compatible wrapper for instructions that: (1) survives simple proxies and client-side transforms, (2) carries proof metadata (who signed/wrapped it), (3) is easy for server to verify without heavy crypto ops in the fast path.
- Keep opt-in for customers; default behaviour is transparent (no changes).
- Support both hosted API and private depl...

text:
Personal experiment log — NOT a finalized policy.

Context:
- Working through messy multi-span / nested-entity cases that the current labeling UI keeps flagging as inconsistent.
- Goal: produce a compact set of heuristics we can apply in a second-pass cleanup job and surface as suggestions in the labeling tool.
- Related: Linear LINK LABEL-1723 (stacked spans), PR in annotation-tool for overlapping spans rendering.

Notes / motivations:
- Many annotators default to 'longest span' which hides useful nested labels (e.g., Product Name + Version + Code identifier). We need a rule set to decide when to keep nested tags vs collapse.
- Tokenization mismatch is the single biggest source of disagreement. Tokenizer: huggingface/llama-tokenizer (v1) in our pipeline; many human labels are character-indexed, the UI converts to tokens poorly for hyphenates and slashes.

Quick heuristics (proposal):...

title: Clauses to surface before signing — quick pocket checklist

text:
PURPOSE:
A compact, pragmatic checklist I can carry into negotiation threads or last-minute review calls. Focus is on high-risk items customers often try to slip in or want changed at signature time, plus one-line negotiation scripts and quick fallbacks to speed triage.

HOW TO USE:
- Run checklist before a final exec signature ask (do not rely on Sales summary alone).
- Mark items H/M/L risk and call Legal if any H items are present.

QUICK TRIAGE (one-line rationale):
- H: Unlimited liability / open-ended indemnities / customer-driven data export w/o controls — blocks signature without Legal/Eng design.
- M: Changes to data residency / audit rights / subprocessors — usually solvable with controls, SLA, or product offer.
- L: Cosmetic edits, definition clarifications, timing tweaks.

TOP ITEMS TO SURFACE (priority order + negotiation script + fallback):
1) Liability cap & carve-outs ...

text:
Context: quick brain dump after the SOC2 pre-audit dry run (late shift). Goal is to capture loose evidence threads, quick mitigations we can do before the audit window, and a short cutover checklist for the on-call coverage during the actual attestation. This is messy, intentionally — use as a handoff to morning crew.

High-level takeaways from dry run:
- Evidence gaps concentrated around ephemeral infra (CI agents, ephemeral keys) and human processes (onboarding docs out-of-date).
- Observability evidence exists but scattered (traces in Splunk, traces in local dev accounts). Need canonical links.
- Incident log continuity: some older tickets were archived without postmortem; need at least summary notes for auditor.

Late-night evidence hunt plan (ordered by impact / time-to-finish):
1) SSO: generate consolidated export of SAML config + admin change history (last 24 months). Easy win:...

title: Prompt Weave Tinkerpad
preview:
Purpose and mental model:
This is my personal tinkerpad for the "weave" approach — building small composable prompt fragments (cues, constraints, neutralizers) and trying to stitch them together into robust few-shot templates. Goal is pragmatic: get reliable behavior across model variants and quantization profiles w...
text:
Purpose and mental model:
This is my personal tinkerpad for the "weave" approach — building small composable prompt fragments (cues, constraints, neutralizers) and trying to stitch them together into robust few-shot templates. Goal is pragmatic: get reliable behavior across model variants and quantization profiles without exploding token budgets.

High-level hypotheses to validate:
- Short, labeled cues + 2 exemplars give more stable instruction-following than 1 long system message for our retrieval-style assistant tasks.
- Explicit "neutralizer" sentences reduce confident hallucinations in domain-limited Q&A (finance/legal) more than lowering temperature alone.
- Interleaving negative exemplars (bad outputs) helps models avoid repeating common failure modes when the domain has many ambiguous entities.

Core concepts (vocabulary used in notes below):
- cue: tiny instruction fragment (...
